title: Rodent Gait Analysis Using Linear Track Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"20ghh","text":"This protocol is used to evaluate quantitative aspects of mouse locomotor behavior (gait).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"92nhp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fl24h","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ickv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"alt":"","guid":"C074B83D76AF11EFBFF00A58A9FEAC02","height":262,"id":500382,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/jpeg","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"Catwalk.jpeg","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/rit9bixhx.jpeg","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/riw8bixhx.jpg","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/riw7bixhx.webp","width":550}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"5feo","text":"Supplies:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5d8n3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1vj9","text":"Home-made catwalk apparatus consisting of clear acrylic walking corridor (33 inches long x 3.25 inches wide) on an acrylic stand (8.25 inches high - which are glued to the side of the base). ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7pq3","text":"Home-made black acrylic “home” box (8 inches width/ 8 inches length/ 8 inches height).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9q8vp","text":"Bedding and enrichment (placed in black “home” box to entice mouse to walk).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"96nvk","text":"Mirror (26.5 inches long x 8.5 inches wide) placed underneath the catwalk.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"53vga","text":"High-speed camera (60 fps or higher) pointed at mirror.\n","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8m4ge","text":"Setup:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6g268","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5lbi6","text":"Clean apparatus with 70% ethanol between animals. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6vt3q","text":"Replace bedding/enrichment in the black “home” box.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: There is no need to habituate mice to the apparatus; mice will walk across voluntarily to enter the black box.
Place mouse on open end of the catwalk; observe to make sure mouse walks towards the black “home” box.
When mouse reaches “home” box, stop video recording.
Remove mouse from “home” box and re-place on the open end.
Repeat this process 4-5 times (or until mouse stops traversing catwalk). A greater number of trials yields a higher number of strides for subsequent analysis.
After final trial, return mouse to home cage.
Data can be analyzed by following the steps described in: https://github.com/UCSF-Nelson-Lab/Catwalk-Analysi
creator: Rose B. Creed
